question: Why is tree planting so important?
answer: Trees clean our air and water, cool our cities, store carbon, prevent soil erosion and provide habitat for wildlife. Planting and protecting trees is one of the most effective and affordable ways to fight climate change and restore ecological balance.

question: How do I select the right tree species to plant?
answer: Choose native species suited to your local soil, rainfall and climate. Native trees support local wildlife and need less maintenance. Our team offers free technical advice to help you pick the right species for your land and purpose.

question: What is the best season for planting trees?
answer: In most of India, the onset of the monsoon (June–August) is ideal, as seedlings get natural rainfall to establish their roots. Planting can also be done in other seasons with adequate irrigation and care.

question: How can I participate in Treelands Foundation's initiatives?
answer: You can volunteer, partner with us on CSR projects, seek our consultancy, or simply reach out for free technical advice on planting and maintaining trees on your land. Visit our Get Involved page to begin.

question: Where can I source quality seedlings?
answer: Quality seedlings should come from reputable nurseries that raise healthy, disease-free native stock. We can guide you to trusted sources and help assess seedling quality.

question: What support does Treelands Foundation offer?
answer: We offer free technical advice to tree planters, planting and maintenance services for landowners at reasonable rates, and full forestry, wildlife and environmental consultancy for institutions and corporates.
